title: alsine purpurea
intro: Alsine purpurea, also known as mountain avens, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of mountain avens include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Mountain avens is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
